title: Microsoft to spend $120B on AI this year, used by 3% of customers
desc: Daily AI-generated tech news satire. Microsoft Q3 earnings + Copilot adoption.

beats.name: capex-rain
beats.narration: The company's filings show a hundred twenty billion dollars in AI infrastructure this year.

beats.name: server-farm-quarter
beats.narration: Thirty-five billion of it, last quarter alone.

beats.name: pull-back-purpose
beats.narration: All of it built to power one product.
beats.cinematography: Slow pull-back already in prompt; no extra

beats.name: copilot-marketing
beats.narration: The AI assistant Microsoft has spent two years promoting.
beats.cinematography: Slow pan right (mimics walking past billboard)

beats.name: 450m-customers
beats.narration: Microsoft three-sixty-five has roughly four hundred fifty million customers.

beats.name: copilot-among-cubicles
beats.narration: Reports suggest only three percent of them currently use Copilot.

beats.name: copilot-alone-3-percent
beats.narration: Three percent. Of four hundred fifty million.
beats.cinematography: Quick zoom-pop on `3%` at narration moment

beats.name: gap-visualization
beats.narration: The gap between what was spent, and what is used.
beats.cinematography: Hard cut with brief 0.1s magenta flash on entry

beats.name: copilot-existential
beats.narration: Microsoft's projected capital expenditure, rises into next quarter.

beats.name: punchline-hero
beats.narration: One hundred twenty billion dollars. Used by almost nobody.
beats.cinematography: Slow hero zoom-in on Copilot (1.0× → 1.05× over full 6s)

sources.attribution: Microsoft FY26 capex guide ($120B for fiscal year)
Microsoft Q3 FY26 filing (analyst expectation $34.9B; rounded for TTS readability)
MSFT Q3 FY26 earnings coverage cycle (TechStartups, multiple analyst reports)
Copilot launched March 2023, GA late 2023; ~2 years promo through Apr 2026
Apr 29, 2026 = Wednesday, FY26 Q3 earnings call confirmed via Microsoft IR

transcript: On Wednesday, Microsoft reported earnings. The company's filings show a hundred twenty billion dollars in AI infrastructure this year. Thirty-five billion of it, last quarter alone. All of it built to power one product. Copilot. The AI assistant Microsoft has spent two years promoting. Microsoft three-sixty-five has roughly four hundred fifty million customers. Reports suggest only three percent of them currently use Copilot. Three percent. Of four hundred fifty million. The gap between what was spent, and what is used. Microsoft's projected capital expenditure, rises into next quarter. One hundred twenty billion dollars. Used by almost nobody.

writing.author: Claude
